title: Spinosa.
content:
A mighty oak here ruined lies,
    Its top was wont to kiss the skies,
     Why is it now o'erthrown?
    The peasants needed, so they said,
    Its wood wherewith to build a shed,
     And so they've cut it down.
meaning:
Friedrich Schiller's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "Spinosa."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
